description:
## Background The LLM generates decklists that violate Magic's fundamental deck construction rules. Example from a real user session: ``` 4x Sunspine Lynx ← main deck ... 4x Sunspine Lynx ← sideboard ``` 8 copies of the same card across the 75. The system prompt says "do not invent cards" but this is also violated — multiple cards in that session were fictional (Burnout Bashtronaut, Hexing Squelcher, Nova Hellkite, Zhao the Moon Slayer, Soulstone Sanctuary). This bead addresses both with a two-layer approach: prompt hardening and a post-generation validator. --- ## Layer 1 — System Prompt Hardening (assemble.ts) Add a `## Deck Construction Rules` section to `RESPONSE_SYSTEM_BASE` in `src/query/assemble.ts`: ``` ## Deck Construction Rules When generating any decklist, you MUST follow these rules. Violations are illegal and will confuse users. COPY LIMIT: A maximum of 4 copies of any card may appear across the combined mainboard + sideboard. Count copies from BOTH zones together before finalizing. Exception: basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and their Snow-Covered and full-art variants) have no copy limit. CORRECT: Main: 4x Shock Side: 2x Shock ← total 6... ILLEGAL Main: 4x Shock Side: 0x Shock ← total 4, legal DECK SIZE: Standard — minimum 60 main, maximum 15 sideboard. Modern — same. CARD EXISTENCE: Only generate real Magic cards that exist in print. If you are not certain a card exists and is legal in the format, do not include it. It is better to use a placeholder comment like "// [add your preferred removal here]" than to invent a card name. FORMAT LEGALITY: Only include cards legal in the format the user is asking about. Do not include banned cards. Before outputting any decklist, mentally count total copies of each non-basic card across main + side. If any card exceeds 4, reduce the sideboard count first. ``` This won't eliminate violations entirely but will catch the most egregious cases where the model loses track of running totals. --- ## Layer 2 — Post-Generation Validator (src/query/decklist.ts) New module with three exported functions. No external dependencies. ### `parseDecklist(text: string): { main: CardEntry[]; side: CardEntry[] } | null` - Searches the LLM response for a markdown code block (` ``` ` fenced) containing decklist content - Parses lines matching: `4x Card Name`, `4 Card Name`, `4x Card Name // comment` - Recognizes section breaks: lines containing "sideboard", "side board", "sb:", "// sideboard" (case-insensitive) switch the parser to sideboard mode - Strips section headers (lines starting with `//` that don't have card counts) - Returns `null` if no parseable decklist is found in the text ```ts type CardEntry = { name: string; qty: number } ``` ### `validateDecklist(main: CardEntry[], side: CardEntry[]): ValidationError[]` - Merges counts across main + side per card name (case-insensitive) - Flags any non-basic-land card with total count > 4 - Flags main deck total < 60 or > 250 (sanity bounds) - Flags sideboard total > 15 ```ts type ValidationError = | { type: 'copy_limit'; card: string; main_qty: number; side_qty: number; total: number } | { type: 'deck_size'; main_total: number } | { type: 'sideboard_size'; side_total: number } ``` Basic land detection: `['plains', 'island', 'swamp', 'mountain', 'forest', 'snow-covered plains', 'snow-covered island', 'snow-covered swamp', 'snow-covered mountain', 'snow-covered forest', 'wastes']` — if name matches (case-insensitive), skip the copy limit check. ### `formatValidationWarning(errors: ValidationError[]): string` - Returns a human-readable string for each error, formatted for display - Example: `"⚠ Decklist issue: 8 copies of Sunspine Lynx found across main and sideboard (max 4). Review before importing to MTGA."` - If multiple errors, bullet-list them --- ## Layer 3 — Wire into the Streaming API Route Location: `src/app/api/query/route.ts` The streaming handler collects delta chunks into a buffer. After the `done` event is processed (full text assembled): 1. Call `parseDecklist(fullText)` 2. If a decklist is found, call `validateDecklist(main, side)` 3. If errors exist, emit a new SSE event before the `done` event: ``` event: decklist_warning data: { "errors": [ { "type": "copy_limit", "card": "Sunspine Lynx", "main_qty": 4, "side_qty": 4, "total": 8 } ], "message": "⚠ Decklist issue: 8 copies of Sunspine Lynx across main and sideboard (max 4)." } ``` This means the frontend receives the warning alongside the complete response, not blocking the stream. --- ## Layer 4 — Frontend Warning Display (src/app/chat/page.tsx) The SSE handler already switches on `currentEvent`. Add a case for `decklist_warning`: - Store the warning message in the oracle message's `meta` object: `meta.decklist_warning: string | null` - After the prose response body, if `msg.meta?.decklist_warning` is set, render: ```tsx
⚠ {msg.meta.decklist_warning}
``` Use `text-flame` (orange-red) — warning, not error. Don't block the response. --- ## What This Does NOT Fix **Hallucinated cards** (e.g., "Burnout Bashtronaut") are a separate problem. Detecting them requires a Scryfall name lookup against the cards table. That's tracked separately — this bead focuses purely on structural rule violations detectable without external DB calls. The copy-limit validator is purely string/count based and runs in O(n) with no DB dependency, making it safe to run on every response. --- ## Files to Touch - `src/query/assemble.ts` — add deck construction rules to RESPONSE_SYSTEM_BASE - `src/query/decklist.ts` — new file: parseDecklist, validateDecklist, formatValidationWarning - `src/app/api/query/route.ts` — wire validator into stream handler, emit decklist_warning event - `src/app/chat/page.tsx` — handle decklist_warning SSE event, render warning in message meta footer --- ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] System prompt includes deck construction rules section with copy limit, basic land exception, deck size - [ ] `parseDecklist` correctly extracts main + side from markdown code blocks in LLM output - [ ] `validateDecklist` detects: copy limit violations (non-basic only), sideboard > 15, main < 60 - [ ] Basic lands (Mountain, Forest, etc.) are exempt from copy limit check - [ ] Copy limit violation in the reproducer case (4x Sunspine Lynx main + 4x side) produces a `copy_limit` error - [ ] `decklist_warning` SSE event is emitted when violations are found - [ ] Warning renders in the chat UI below the response, in flame color - [ ] Responses without decklists are unaffected (no false positives) - [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes - [ ] Unit tests for parseDecklist and validateDecklist covering: valid deck (no warning), copy limit violation, basic land exemption, sideboard overflow, no decklist in response
title: Enforce decklist correctness — 4-copy rule validator and prompt hardening

description:
Navigating directly to /data/modern/[archetype-id] (e.g. /data/modern/mono-black-tron-modern) shows empty content — all Suspense sections render their empty states. Navigating client-side (homepage → /data/modern → click archetype in MetaShareBar) loads the page correctly.

ROOT CAUSE (high confidence):
src/app/data/[format]/[archetype]/queries.ts uses createStaticClient() for all data fetching — including the runtime Server Component queries (ArchetypeHeader, ShareOverTimeSection, RecentResultsSection, WinRateOverTimeSection). The comment on createStaticClient explicitly says it is for 'build-time / static generation contexts (generateStaticParams, generateMetadata) where cookies() is not available.'

Client-side navigation works because it serves the pre-rendered RSC payload from generateStaticParams (which ran createStaticClient at build time — the correct context). Direct navigation triggers a fresh server render. In that context, createStaticClient() uses bare @supabase/supabase-js with no explicit fetch cache config, which may interact badly with Next.js App Router's fetch deduplication/caching and return empty results.

The format overview page (src/app/data/[format]/queries.ts) uses createClient() from @supabase/ssr throughout and does not have this issue.

FIX:
In src/app/data/[format]/[archetype]/queries.ts:
- Keep createStaticClient() only in fetchAllArchetypeIds() (used by generateStaticParams) and fetchArchetype() when called from generateMetadata
- Switch all other query functions (fetchLatestSnapshot, fetchShareHistory, fetchHasMatches, fetchRecentResults, and fetchArchetype when called at runtime) to use createClient() from @/lib/supabase-server

The cleanest approach: change all query functions to accept a supabase client as a parameter, pass createStaticClient() from generateStaticParams/generateMetadata and createClient() from runtime Server Components. Or simply switch all runtime queries to createClient() — cookies are available in all Server Component render contexts.

ALSO INVESTIGATE:
If the fix above doesn't resolve it, check whether the archetype_id in the URL matches the archetype_id in metagame_snapshots. A mismatch (e.g. archetypes table has 'mono-black-tron-modern' but snapshots reference a different id) would cause the archetype header to render (fetchArchetype finds a row) but all data sections to be empty (no matching snapshots/deck_archetypes rows).

REPRO STEPS:
1. Navigate directly to /data/modern/mono-black-tron-modern (or any archetype slug)
2. Observe: all sections show empty states
3. Navigate to / → /data/modern → click the same archetype in the MetaShareBar
4. Observe: page loads correctly with full data

ACCEPTANCE:
Direct navigation and client-side navigation to the same archetype URL produce identical content.

description: intent.ts line 41: return parsed as Intent with only a typeof object check. If the LLM returns timeframe_days: undefined or an invalid value, 86_400_000 * undefined = NaN, producing an Invalid Date cutoff that breaks all range queries silently. Fix: normalize timeframe_days to one of [30,60,90] defaulting to 90, normalize format to one of ['modern','standard'] or null.

description:
## What This Is

A parallel product vertical targeting Commander/EDH players — the largest and fastest-growing segment of Magic players. Unlike Modern/Standard where the oracle answers "what's winning?", the EDH oracle answers "what should I build, and how does it play at my table?"

Commander is fundamentally a social format. The questions players ask are different:
- "Generate me a Bracket 3 Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver list for $80"
- "What are the best cards for a Zombie tribal sacrifice deck?"
- "Does my deck have infinite combos? What bracket does it land in?"
- "What commanders pair well with a heavy graveyard theme?"
- "What's the fastest way to win with Tymna/Thrasios in cEDH?"

No existing tool answers these conversationally. EDHRec is a database. Moxfield is a deck builder. Commander Spellbook is a combo lookup. Firemind can be the intelligent layer on top of all of them.

---

## Why Now

WotC introduced the Commander Bracket system (Brackets 1–4) in early 2025. The community is actively adopting it but has no tools to programmatically score a deck against brackets. This is a live, unmet need with viral potential — a "what bracket is my deck?" calculator could be a significant acquisition driver.

---

## Product Pillars

### 1. Bracket-Aware Deck Generation
The centerpiece. Users can ask for decks with explicit constraints:
- Bracket level (1–4)
- Budget ($X paper, Y tix)
- Commander name
- Theme or strategy
- Color identity

Example prompt: "Build me a Bracket 2 Kenrith, the Returned King deck with a lifegain theme under $60."

The oracle generates a 99-card list that respects the bracket constraints, uses statistically popular cards for that commander (EDHRec data), avoids Game Changers if below bracket 4, and flags any infinite combos present.

### 2. Bracket Scoring / Analyzer
Given a decklist pasted into chat, score it against the bracket rubric:
- Detect Game Changers (WotC published list)
- Detect infinite combos via Commander Spellbook
- Detect mass land denial, extra-turn chains
- Return: "Your deck is Bracket 3 — it contains 2 Game Changers (Rhystic Study, Cyclonic Rift) and 1 infinite combo (Gravecrawler + Phyrexian Altar + Wilhelt)."

This is deterministic scoring, not LLM hallucination — the lists are known.

### 3. Commander Intelligence Chat
Everything the oracle does for Modern/Standard, but for Commander:
- Card recommendations for a given commander + strategy
- Combo discovery ("what infinite combos does Wilhelt enable?")
- Budget comparisons across builds
- Commander discovery ("what's a good black/white commander for a token build under Bracket 3?")
- cEDH tier list queries ("what are the strongest Bracket 4/cEDH commanders right now?")

### 4. Commander Data Pages (Frontend — Phase EDH-3)
Parallel to `/data/modern` — a `/data/commander` section with:
- Top 50 commanders by deck count
- Per-commander page: top cards, top combos, bracket distribution, avg price
- Theme filtering (Sacrifice, Tribal, Voltron, Stax, etc.)

---

## Data Sources

### EDHRec JSON API (primary)
EDHRec aggregates deck data from Moxfield and Archidekt. Their internal JSON endpoints are publicly accessible without authentication — the same endpoints their own website uses:

```
https://json.edhrec.com/pages/commanders/wilhelt-the-rotcleaver.json
https://json.edhrec.com/pages/commanders/wilhelt-the-rotcleaver/themes/tribal.json
https://json.edhrec.com/pages/top/commanders.json
```

Returns: card inclusion percentages, synergy scores, avg deck counts, theme breakdowns.

This is a scrape/ingestion play — pull on a weekly cadence, store in Supabase. No official API key needed. Legal grey area similar to MTGO/Goldfish scraping. Mitigation: be a good citizen (rate limit, cache aggressively, don't hit continuously).

**Scope to ingest:** Top 300 commanders by deck count covers ~80% of the player base. Weekly refresh.

### Commander Spellbook (combo database)
Documented public API. Returns combo "variants" — sets of cards producing a given effect.

```
GET https://backend.commanderspellbook.com/api/variants/?q=commander%3AWilhelt
```

Returns: ingredient cards, combo result (e.g., "Infinite tokens"), steps, bracket-relevant flags.

Pull on weekly cadence, store combos in Supabase. This powers both the bracket scorer and the combo intelligence in chat.

### WotC Game Changers List
Static list of cards that escalate a deck's bracket (published by WotC when they announced brackets). Ingest once, update when WotC updates the list. Powers the bracket scorer.

Cards include: Cyclonic Rift, Rhystic Study, Smothering Tithe, Dockside Extortionist, Thassa's Oracle, Demonic Tutor, Vampiric Tutor, etc. (~50 cards total).

### Scryfall (already have)
Already ingested weekly. Powers: budget filtering (prices), color identity validation, card type/oracle text for combo detection.

---

## Schema Extensions

New tables alongside the existing ones:

```sql
commanders
  id           text PK     -- edhrec slug (e.g., "wilhelt-the-rotcleaver")
  name         text
  color_identity text[]    -- ['B', 'U'] etc.
  type_line    text
  deck_count   int         -- from edhrec
  edhrec_rank  int
  updated_at   timestamptz

commander_cards             -- top cards for each commander (EDHRec inclusions)
  commander_id text FK
  card_name    text
  inclusion_pct numeric     -- 0-100
  synergy_score numeric     -- EDHRec synergy metric
  themes       text[]       -- which themes this card appears in
  updated_at   timestamptz
  UNIQUE(commander_id, card_name)

commander_combos            -- from Commander Spellbook
  id           text PK      -- spellbook variant ID
  commander_id text FK
  cards        text[]       -- ingredient card names
  result       text         -- "Infinite tokens" etc.
  steps        text[]
  bracket_relevant boolean  -- does this combo make the deck Bracket 4?

game_changers               -- WotC published list
  card_name    text PK
  reason       text         -- why it's a game changer
  added_at     date
```

---

## Intelligence Layer Changes

### Intent Extraction
Update `extractIntent()` to detect EDH queries:
- New fields: `commander_name`, `bracket`, `budget_usd`, `edh_theme`, `format = 'commander'`
- Detect bracket mentions ("bracket 2", "bracket 3", "casual", "cEDH")
- Detect commander names (hard: any creature could be a commander — use Scryfall `can_be_commander` tag or legend check)

### EDH Retrieval
New `retrieveCommanderContext()` parallel to existing `retrieveContext()`:
1. Resolve commander name → `commanders` table
2. Pull top 40 cards from `commander_cards` (filtered by theme if specified)
3. Pull combos from `commander_combos`
4. Pull game changers intersection with top cards
5. Apply bracket filter (exclude Game Changers if bracket < 4, exclude combo-finishing pieces if bracket < 3)
6. Apply budget filter using Scryfall prices

### Deck Generation Prompt
New system prompt section for EDH deck generation:
- 99-card constraint (commander is the 100th)
- Bracket rules baked in (Game Changer thresholds per bracket)
- Basic land backfill to 99
- Mana curve guidance from EDHRec averages
- Scryfall format legality check (commander banlist)

---

## Phases

### Phase EDH-1: Data Foundation (P2)
- EDHRec scraper + ingestion pipeline (top 300 commanders, weekly)
- Commander Spellbook ingestion (combos)
- Game Changers list ingestion
- Schema migrations

### Phase EDH-2: Intelligence (P2)
- Intent extraction: EDH query detection, commander name resolution
- EDH retrieval context assembly
- Bracket scorer (deterministic, not LLM)
- Update chat pipeline to route EDH queries to EDH retrieval

### Phase EDH-3: Frontend (P3)
- /data/commander overview
- Per-commander pages (/data/commander/[slug])
- Bracket calculator UI (paste a list, get a bracket score — viral potential)

---

## Competitive Positioning

| Feature | EDHRec | Moxfield | Archidekt | Firemind EDH |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Card recommendations | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Bracket scoring | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Conversational / AI | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Combo detection | partial | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Budget-constrained builds | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| cEDH tier info | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Deck building UI | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Social / community | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |

Firemind doesn't try to replace Moxfield as a deck builder — it's the intelligence layer that makes building better. Natural flow: ask Firemind for a Bracket 3 Wilhelt list → paste it into Moxfield to build and share.

---

## Open Questions / Decisions Needed

1. **EDHRec ToS / scraping risk** — their data is aggregated from Moxfield/Archidekt (public decks). Using their JSON endpoints is grey. Should we reach out to EDHRec about a data partnership, or proceed with scraping and handle any takedown if it comes? Given our scale, scraping is likely fine for now.

2. **cEDH scope** — cEDH has actual tournament data (tournaments.mtgpowerhouse.com, cEDH Decklist Database). Should Phase EDH-1 include cEDH tournament scraping (parallel to MTGO for competitive formats)? This would make the oracle more data-grounded for cEDH queries rather than relying on EDHRec deck counts.

3. **Bracket 5 / house rules** — WotC's system goes to 4 (5 is unofficial cEDH). Should we support bracket 5 / "Rule 0" as a first-class concept?

4. **Commander name resolution** — any legendary creature can be a commander. Intent extraction needs to identify commander names from free text ("build me a wilhelt list" → Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver). Options: Scryfall fuzzy search, hardcode top 300 commander names, or LLM extraction with validation.
title: EDH Epic — Commander intelligence vertical

description:
## Context
Child of mindsculptor-3w3 (EDH Epic). Data foundation for all EDH intelligence features. No frontend or LLM changes in this bead — pure ingestion pipeline.

## What to Build

### 1. Schema migrations
Four new tables (see epic for full column definitions):
- `commanders` — top commanders by EDHRec rank
- `commander_cards` — per-commander card inclusions + synergy scores
- `commander_combos` — combo variants from Commander Spellbook
- `game_changers` — WotC bracket escalator card list

Add indexes: `commander_cards(commander_id, inclusion_pct DESC)` for fast top-N lookups.

### 2. Game Changers list (one-time seed)
Hardcode WotC's published Game Changers list as a seed migration. Known cards include:
Cyclonic Rift, Rhystic Study, Smothering Tithe, Dockside Extortionist, Thassa's Oracle, Demonic Tutor, Vampiric Tutor, Imperial Seal, Necropotence, Ad Nauseam, Deflecting Swipe, Fierce Guardianship, Counterspell (not a GC but check list), Jeweled Lotus, Mana Crypt, Sol Ring (check — may not be GC), Mystical Tutor, Personal Tutor, Survival of the Fittest, Sylvan Library, Seedborn Muse, Teferi's Protection, Armageddon, Ravages of War, Winter Orb, Static Orb, Stasis.
Cross-check against: https://magic.wizards.com/en/news/announcements/introducing-commander-brackets-beta

### 3. EDHRec scraper (`src/scrapers/edhrec.ts`)
Fetches EDHRec commander data via their internal JSON endpoints (no auth required):

```
GET https://json.edhrec.com/pages/top/commanders.json
  → rank, name, deck count for top commanders

GET https://json.edhrec.com/pages/commanders/{slug}.json
  → card list with inclusion_pct, synergy_score, labels (themes)
```

Scrape top 300 commanders by deck count. For each commander, fetch card inclusions. Parse and upsert into `commanders` + `commander_cards`.

Rate limit: 1 req/sec, respect any 429s with exponential backoff. Do not run continuously — weekly cadence only.

Store raw JSON in `scrape_jobs` (source = 'edhrec') before parsing, consistent with existing pipeline pattern.

### 4. Commander Spellbook scraper (`src/scrapers/spellbook.ts`)
Public documented API:
```
GET https://backend.commanderspellbook.com/api/variants/?q=commander%3A{slug}&format=json
```

For each commander in our DB, fetch combo variants. Parse ingredient cards, result description, step list. Upsert into `commander_combos`.

The API supports pagination — handle `next` link in response.

### 5. EDH parse worker (`src/parsers/edhrec.ts`)
Normalizes `scrape_jobs` rows (source = 'edhrec') → `commanders` + `commander_cards`. Follows same scrape_jobs → parse → upsert pattern as existing MTGO parser.

### 6. Cron wiring
Add `sync-edhrec` cron to `render.yaml` — weekly (Sunday 4am UTC). Runs EDHRec + Spellbook scrapers.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four tables exist in Supabase with correct indexes
- [ ] Game Changers list seeded (verify against WotC announcement)
- [ ] Top 300 commanders in `commanders` table with deck_count and edhrec_rank
- [ ] `commander_cards` populated for each commander (top 100 cards per commander minimum)
- [ ] `commander_combos` populated for commanders with known combos
- [ ] Scraper respects rate limits (1 req/sec), stores raw in scrape_jobs before parsing
- [ ] Weekly cron wired in render.yaml
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
title: EDH data foundation — EDHRec ingestion, Spellbook combos, Game Changers seed

description:
## Context

`src/query/decklist.ts` currently validates 60-card format rules only (max 4 copies, min 60 main, max 15 sideboard). Commander has completely different rules. This bead extends the validator to support EDH without breaking the existing 60-card path.

`color_identity text[]` already exists on the `cards` table (Scryfall sync). No schema changes needed.

---

## Changes to `src/query/decklist.ts`

### New error types

Add to the `ValidationError` union:

```ts
| { type: 'edh_singleton'; card: string; qty: number }
| { type: 'edh_deck_size'; main_total: number }
| { type: 'color_identity_violation'; card: string; card_identity: string[]; commander_identity: string[] }
```

### New `ValidationOptions` type

```ts
export type ValidationOptions = {
  format?: '60-card' | 'commander'
  // Commander-only: the commander's color identity e.g. ['B', 'U']
  commanderColorIdentity?: string[]
  // Commander-only: pre-resolved map of card name (lowercase) → color identity
  // Caller resolves from Supabase before calling validateDecklist
  cardColorIdentities?: Map<string, string[]>
}
```

### Update `validateDecklist` signature

```ts
export function validateDecklist(
  main: CardEntry[],
  side: CardEntry[],
  options: ValidationOptions = {}
): ValidationError[]
```

### Commander format rules (when `options.format === 'commander'`)

Replace the existing 60-card checks with Commander-specific checks:

**Deck size**: Main should be exactly 99 cards (the commander is the 100th and lives outside the list). Warn if `mainTotal !== 99`. Use a tolerance: warn if < 95 or > 101 to avoid false positives from LLM miscounts.

**No sideboard**: Commander has no sideboard. If `side.length > 0`, emit a `sideboard_size` error with a commander-specific message.

**Singleton rule**: Max 1 copy of any non-basic card across the entire deck (no sideboard to check since we error on sideboard above). Same BASIC_LANDS exemption applies. Emit `edh_singleton` for any card with `qty > 1`.

**Color identity**: If `commanderColorIdentity` and `cardColorIdentities` are both provided, check every non-basic card. A card violates color identity if any of its identity colors are not in the commander's identity. Emit `color_identity_violation`.

The 60-card checks (copy_limit ≤ 4, deck_size ≥ 60, sideboard ≤ 15) only run when format is `'60-card'` or unspecified.

### Update `fixCopyLimits` → `fixDeckErrors`

Rename (or add alongside) a function that handles both modes:
- 60-card: reduce copies to 4 (existing behavior)
- Commander: reduce copies to 1, remove sideboard entirely, remove color identity violations (replace with a comment line `// [replace: color identity violation]` so the user knows something was removed rather than silently disappearing)

### Update `formatValidationWarning` for new error types

```ts
case 'edh_singleton':
  return `${e.qty} copies of ${e.card} (Commander allows only 1)`
case 'edh_deck_size':
  return `Deck has ${e.main_total} cards (Commander main deck should be 99)`
case 'color_identity_violation':
  return `${e.card} is outside your commander's color identity (${e.commander_identity.join('')})`
```

---

## Changes to `src/app/api/query/route.ts`

### Format detection

After the stream completes and `fullAnswer` is assembled, the route already calls `parseDecklist`. Extend this block:

1. Determine format: use `result.intent.format === 'commander'` if intent is available, otherwise fall back to heuristic: `main.length` near 99 and no sideboard → `'commander'`.

2. If commander format, resolve color identities from Supabase:

```ts
async function resolveColorIdentities(
  cards: string[],
  supabase: SupabaseClient
): Promise<Map<string, string[]>> {
  const { data } = await supabase
    .from('cards')
    .select('name, color_identity')
    .in('name', cards)

  const map = new Map<string, string[]>()
  for (const row of data ?? []) {
    map.set(row.name.toLowerCase(), row.color_identity ?? [])
  }
  return map
}
```

3. Extract commander's color identity: parse the decklist for a "Commander:" section (MTGA export format includes it), or look it up from `cards` table by matching the single legendary creature at qty 1. If it can't be determined, skip color identity validation (don't false-positive).

4. Pass `{ format: 'commander', commanderColorIdentity, cardColorIdentities }` to `validateDecklist`.

---

## Commander section parsing in `parseDecklist`

MTGA copy format for Commander includes:

```
Commander:
1 Wilhelt, the Rotcleaver

Deck:
4 ...
```

Update `parseDecklist` to recognize the "Commander:" / "Deck:" section headers and extract the commander card separately. Return it in the result:

```ts
// Updated return type
{ main: CardEntry[]; side: CardEntry[]; commander?: CardEntry }
```

The commander card is NOT included in the main count (it's the 100th card). Use it to look up color identity in the route.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 60-card validation behavior unchanged — no regressions
- [ ] Commander deck with duplicate non-basic emits `edh_singleton` error
- [ ] Commander deck with 97 cards emits `edh_deck_size` warning
- [ ] Commander deck with a sideboard emits `sideboard_size` error
- [ ] Color identity violation detected and reported (e.g., Forest in a mono-black Wilhelt deck)
- [ ] Basic lands exempt from singleton rule (20x Mountain in a Krenko list is fine)
- [ ] Format detected correctly from intent OR heuristic (99 cards, no sideboard)
- [ ] Commander card parsed from "Commander:" section if present
- [ ] Color identity lookup skipped gracefully if commander can't be determined
- [ ] `fixDeckErrors` in commander mode reduces duplicates to 1 and removes color violations
- [ ] Warning message in chat UI reads clearly for all new error types
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] Unit tests cover: valid EDH deck, singleton violation, color identity violation, basic land exemption, 60-card deck unaffected
title: EDH decklist validation — singleton rule and color identity enforcement
Decklist validator regression tests — 60-card and EDH paths with isolation
Phase 3 audit — UX review, deck list format, full user flow

description:
The empty state structure is correct (centered, query counter present) but the visual energy is too low. Three targeted fixes:

1. Ambient glow opacity: in chat/page.tsx the radial gradient is rgba(79,142,247,0.04) — 4% is invisible. Increase to rgba(79,142,247,0.07) so it registers subtly without being heavy.

2. Prompt chip text brightness: the Button secondary variant uses text-ash by default, making chips look like muted gray boxes. Add text-ink/80 to the chip className so text reads as bright at rest, not just on hover. Current: className='justify-start text-left bg-surface hover:bg-edge px-4 py-3.5 hover:glow-spark-sm'. Add text-ink/80 to that.

3. Subtitle opacity: 'Ask about the current metagame...' is text-ink/60. Bump to text-ink/75 — slightly more presence without competing with the chips.

4. Verify hover:glow-spark-sm is rendering correctly on chip hover in the browser (Tailwind JIT custom class with variant). If not, replace with an explicit hover:box-shadow via a wrapper or inline style.

5. Note for engineering: the '5 queries available today' counter is hardcoded. Wire it to actual remaining query count from the user session once auth + rate limiting (mindsculptor-toy.7) is complete.
title: Design: empty state polish — brightness and energy

description:
When the article sync worker detects API failures (api_failures > 0 in SYNC_COMPLETE), send a webhook notification to alert us.

## Context
Both scrapers (MTGGoldfish and TCGPlayer) now emit structured SYNC_COMPLETE logs with an api_failures counter. If the upstream API changes or starts blocking us, api_failures will be >0 on consecutive runs. Currently there's no alerting — we'd only notice by manually checking Render logs.

## Requirements
1. At the end of each scraper run, if api_failures > 0 OR errors > threshold, POST a webhook with details (source, counts, timestamp)
2. Support Discord or Slack webhook URL via env var (e.g. ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL)
3. If ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL is not set, skip silently (no crash)
4. Include scraper name, api_failures count, error count, and a human-readable summary in the payload
5. Rate limit: don't fire on every single run if the issue persists — consider a simple "only alert if previous run was healthy" debounce, or just accept the noise for now (P4, keep it simple)

## Non-goals
- No external logging service (Datadog, Logtail, etc.) — just a webhook
- No dashboard or UI — just a notification

## Files likely touched
- src/scrapers/mtggoldfish-articles.ts — call alert function after SYNC_COMPLETE
- src/scrapers/tcgplayer-articles.ts — call alert function after SYNC_COMPLETE  
- src/lib/alert.ts — new, shared webhook helper
- render.yaml — add ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL env var to sync-articles cron
title: Add webhook alerting for article scraper failures

description:
The /chat empty state is a massive black void with prompt chips floating in the middle. It looks abandoned. Fix:

1. Center content vertically in the available space between nav and input bar (use flexbox column with justify-content: center). The prompt chips should be in the visual middle of the screen, not the top third.
2. Add a subtle ambient glow behind the prompt area — a radial gradient from rgba(79,142,247,0.04) to transparent, centered on the chip cluster. Creates a faint 'portal' effect without being heavy.
3. The subtitle 'Ask about the current metagame...' is fine but increase opacity. Muted text at this size almost disappears.
4. Prompt chips: 2x2 grid layout is good. Increase chip padding (more breathing room), add surface background (#0C1220), visible border (#172035), and hover state (border shifts to #4F8EF7 with faint glow). They should look like something you'd want to click.
5. Add a small query counter above the input bar in the empty state: '5 queries available today' (Casual) — makes the product feel alive even before the user types.

Acceptance: the empty state feels intentional and inviting, not like an empty page waiting to load.
title: Design: oracle empty state redesign

description:
The History button exists in the Nav (opens a Drawer) and is stubbed with "Coming in toy.8". The tiers.md spec references history gating (Casual: 7-day lookback, Spike: unlimited) but chat history has no implementation plan yet. Before speccing the engineering work, answer these product questions:

1. **What is a "history" unit?** A full conversation thread (multi-turn), or individual oracle responses? The current chat is a single continuous thread per session — do sessions need explicit names/titles, or are they just timestamped?

2. **Where does history live?** The existing History drawer in the Nav (good for quick access, constrained space) or a dedicated `/chat/history` page (better for browsing many sessions), or both?

3. **Persistence model.** Messages are currently held in React state and lost on refresh. A `CHAT_STORAGE_KEY` localStorage hack exists only for OAuth redirect preservation. Real history needs server-side storage. Options: (a) `chat_sessions` + `chat_messages` tables in Supabase, (b) single `chat_sessions` table with messages as a JSONB column (simpler, not queryable). Which direction?

4. **Gating.** tiers.md says Casual = 7-day lookback, Spike = unlimited. Does anonymous get any history (probably not — no identity to attach it to)? Does history save passively (every query auto-saved) or does the user explicitly save a conversation?

5. **Resume behavior.** Can a user click a past conversation and continue it? Or is history read-only (view old responses but always start fresh)? If resumable, does it count against their daily query limit?

6. **Session boundaries.** What ends a session? Tab close? 30 minutes of inactivity? Explicit "New chat" button? This affects how threads are grouped in the history view.

Once these are answered, the engineering work likely splits into: (a) DB schema + persistence layer, (b) history drawer/page UI, (c) tier-gated access logic.
title: Chat history — product decisions needed before speccing

description:
## Arcana Theme — "Spell Tome"

A fourth theme for users who want full immersion. High-fantasy aesthetic: weathered parchment, flowing arcane typography, selective purple glow effects.

### Visual Design

**Color palette:**
- `--color-canvas`: `244 232 201` (parchment #F4E8C9)
- `--color-surface`: `235 220 185` (aged parchment #EBDCB9)
- `--color-edge`: `180 155 110` (worn leather border)
- `--color-spark`: `124 58 237` (arcane purple #7C3AED)
- `--color-flame`: `220 38 38` (blood red)
- `--color-gold`: `161 98 7` (burnished gold)
- `--color-copper`: `146 64 14` (aged copper)
- `--color-ink`: `28 20 12` (dark brown ink)
- `--color-ash`: `101 75 46` (faded ink)

**Background texture:**
- Replace blueprint grid with CSS radial-gradient age-spot pattern
- Subtle vignette at edges via box-shadow inset on body or radial-gradient overlay
- No grid lines — organic, worn feel

### Typography

**New CSS variables (added in mindsculptor-usx):**
- `--font-display`: controls heading font family
- `--font-body`: controls body/prose font family
- `--text-glow`: CSS drop-shadow or text-shadow value for selective glow effects

**Arcana values:**
- `--font-display`: `'Cinzel', 'Palatino Linotype', Georgia, serif`
- `--font-body`: `'IM Fell English', 'Garamond', 'Palatino Linotype', serif`
- `--text-glow`: `0 0 8px rgb(124 58 237 / 0.6), 0 0 16px rgb(124 58 237 / 0.3)`

**Google Fonts to load (via next/font or <link> in layout):**
- Cinzel (weights 400, 700)
- IM Fell English (weight 400, italic variant)

**Usage:** Apply `--font-display` to h1/h2/h3 via Tailwind class (e.g., `font-display`), `--font-body` to prose content. Other themes use system defaults (--font-display: inherit, --font-body: inherit).

**Selective glow:** Apply `text-glow` utility class (defined in globals.css) only to: spark-colored numbers (meta share %, tier badges), trend arrows, key stat values. NOT applied globally — too heavy-handed.

### Implementation Scope

1. Add `[data-theme="arcana"]` block to globals.css with all CSS variables
2. Wire up background texture (radial-gradient replacing blueprint grid — conditional on theme)
3. Load Cinzel + IM Fell English via next/font — conditional import or via CSS @import scoped to arcana
4. Add `font-display` and `font-body` Tailwind utilities using `--font-display` and `--font-body` variables
5. Add `text-glow` utility class using `--text-glow` variable
6. Apply `font-display` to headings (h1-h3), `font-body` to `.prose-oracle` content when theme is arcana
7. Apply `text-glow` selectively to spark-colored stat values

### Dependencies
- mindsculptor-usx must include `--font-display`, `--font-body`, `--text-glow` variables (all themes define them; non-arcana themes set them to `inherit` / `none`)
- mindsculptor-5ae must be complete (theme switcher UI exists, `data-theme` mechanism works)

### Out of Scope
- No animated effects (candle flicker, particle effects) — future P4 bead if ever
- No custom icons or SVG ornaments in this phase
title: Arcana theme — spell tome aesthetic (Cinzel + parchment + arcane glow)
SQL retrieval layer — query tournaments/decks based on intent

description:
Several components are using off-spec colors. Fix the following:

1. Trend arrows (↑ ↓ → on landing and metagame pages): currently using generic green (#22c55e) and red. Use design system colors: upward trend = #4F8EF7 (electric blue), downward trend = #D4552A (flame-red), neutral → = #4A5878 (muted). The green reads as generic and clashes with the Izzet palette.
2. User message bubbles in /chat: currently unstyled plain bubble. Give them a surface background (#172035), slightly rounded corners, and right-aligned with padding. Should feel like a distinct authored message, not a plain text label.
3. 'See metagame charts →' ghost button on landing: barely visible. Increase border opacity to #4A5878 at rest, text to #E4EEFF. On hover, border shifts to #4F8EF7. Needs to be a real secondary action, not an afterthought.
4. MEDIUM / HIGH / LOW confidence badge: the gold color (#C9A050) is correct for MEDIUM — keep it. Verify LOW uses #D4552A (flame-red) and HIGH uses #4F8EF7 (electric blue) for a clear signal hierarchy.
5. The 'Full breakdown →' link color in the metagame section (landing): currently electric blue — that's correct, keep it.

Acceptance: no green in the UI anywhere. Trend indicators, user bubbles, and confidence badges all use the design system palette.
title: Design: component color corrections

description:
Theme switcher UI and theme definitions. Allows users to switch between Izzet Dark (default), Blueprint (light), and Terminal (green-on-black). Persists to localStorage. Requires mindsculptor-usx (CSS variable refactor) to ship first.

---

## THREE THEMES

### Izzet Dark (default)
The current design. Deep navy-black backgrounds, electric blue accent, Izzet flame-red secondary.
Theme key: 'dark'

### Blueprint (light)
Blueprint paper aesthetic — maintains the schematic/analytical vibe in light mode.
Cool light blue-gray background (#EFF2F8), not pure white. Preserves the 'technical document' feeling.
Deep navy text. Deeper blue accent (#2563EB) for contrast on white — same family as spark, still Izzet.
Theme key: 'light'

### Terminal (dark, green-on-black)
Retro terminal aesthetic. Appeals to the MTGO-playing grinder demographic.
Near-black with green tint background. Terminal green (#22C55E) replaces electric blue as primary accent.
Amber (#F97316) replaces flame-red. Light green-white text.
Theme key: 'terminal'

(The color values are defined in mindsculptor-usx. This bead owns the switcher UI only.)

---

## THEME HOOK

Create src/hooks/useTheme.ts:

  const THEMES = ['dark', 'light', 'terminal'] as const
  type Theme = typeof THEMES[number]

  export function useTheme() {
    const [theme, setThemeState] = useState<Theme>('dark')

    useEffect(() => {
      // Read from DOM (set by FOBT inline script) — source of truth
      const t = document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-theme') as Theme || 'dark'
      setThemeState(t)
    }, [])

    function setTheme(t: Theme) {
      document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', t)
      localStorage.setItem('fm_theme', t)
      setThemeState(t)
    }

    return { theme, setTheme, themes: THEMES }
  }

Note: initial state is 'dark' to match SSR — the useEffect syncs to the actual value from localStorage/FOBT.
This avoids hydration mismatches.

---

## THEME SWITCHER COMPONENT

Create src/components/ThemeSwitcher.tsx

A small icon button in the nav that opens a dropdown popover with theme options.

### Trigger button
- Icon: a circle split into dark/light halves (☯ feel, but custom SVG) — or use a simple palette icon
- 16x16px, text-ash, hover:text-ink
- aria-label="Switch theme"
- Positioned in the nav between the nav links and the auth section (see Nav placement below)

### Dropdown popover
- Opens below the trigger on click, closes on outside click or Escape
- Width: ~180px
- Three rows, one per theme:

  ┌──────────────────────────────┐
  │ ◉ Izzet Dark       ●●●●●    │  ← active checkmark + color swatches
  │   Blueprint        ○○○○○    │
  │   Terminal         ●●●●●    │
  └──────────────────────────────┘

### Color swatches (per row)
5 small circles (8px each, 4px gap) showing the palette:
  canvas → surface → edge → spark → ink
These give an instant visual preview without needing labels.

### Row styling
- Active theme: text-ink, checkmark icon (✓) left of name
- Inactive: text-ash, no checkmark, hover:text-ink hover:bg-surface
- Swatch circles: inline-flex, rounded-full, border border-edge/30

### Swatch colors
Since CSS variables are active, the swatch circles for the current theme naturally show the right colors.
For inactive themes, hardcode the swatch colors as inline styles (they don't use Tailwind tokens):

  Izzet Dark:  ['#06080F','#0C1220','#172035','#4F8EF7','#E4EEFF']
  Blueprint:   ['#EFF2F8','#FFFFFF','#CDD4E8','#2563EB','#0F172A']
  Terminal:    ['#0A0F0A','#0F160F','#1A2E1A','#22C55E','#DCFCE7']

---

## NAV PLACEMENT

Update src/components/Nav.tsx. Add ThemeSwitcher between the nav links section and the auth section:

  BEFORE:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]                    [Sign in] [Go Spike ↑]

  AFTER:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]         [◑] [Sign in] [Go Spike ↑]

  The ◑ icon is the ThemeSwitcher trigger. It sits between the left nav and the right auth cluster.
  Use ml-auto on the ThemeSwitcher's wrapper to push it right, then the auth buttons follow naturally.

Authenticated nav:
  [⚡ Firemind] [Chat][Data]    [◑] [🕐][🔔][avatar▾]

---

## SETTINGS PAGE

Also expose the theme switcher in /settings (when that page exists) as a labeled setting row:

  Appearance
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │ Theme       [Izzet Dark ▾] (dropdown)       │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘

This gives a more explicit, labeled control for users who don't notice the nav icon.
For now (no /settings page yet): nav-only is sufficient.

---

## PERSISTENCE & SSR BEHAVIOR

- Theme stored in localStorage key 'fm_theme'
- FOBT inline script (from mindsculptor-usx) ensures correct theme on initial load — no flash
- useTheme hook reads from DOM attribute (set by FOBT script) on mount to sync React state
- SSR always renders with dark theme (matches :root defaults in CSS) — the inline script corrects
  any non-dark preference before first paint

---

## ADDING FUTURE THEMES

To add a 4th theme:
  1. Add CSS variables for [data-theme="newtheme"] in globals.css
  2. Add 'newtheme' to the THEMES array in useTheme.ts
  3. Add swatch colors to ThemeSwitcher.tsx
That's it — no other changes needed.

---

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] useTheme hook reads from DOM on mount, no hydration mismatch
- [ ] setTheme updates data-theme attribute, localStorage, and React state atomically
- [ ] ThemeSwitcher renders in nav, trigger is accessible (aria-label)
- [ ] Dropdown shows all 3 themes with correct swatches and active checkmark
- [ ] Clicking a theme applies it instantly (no reload)
- [ ] Selected theme persists across page navigation and browser refresh
- [ ] Blueprint theme: all text is readable on light backgrounds (no white-on-white)
- [ ] Terminal theme: all text is readable on dark green backgrounds
- [ ] Opacity modifiers still work (bg-canvas/80 etc.) in all three themes
- [ ] npx tsc --noEmit passes
title: Theme switcher — Izzet Dark, Blueprint, Terminal

description:
The page background and content surfaces are nearly identical darkness, making the UI feel flat and gloomy. Fix:

2. Nav bar: add a bottom border at #172035 so it separates from page content. Currently bleeds into the page.
3. Metagame section on landing page: wrap in a surface-colored container (#0C1220) with a subtle border (#172035). Right now it floats on the same black as the background with no visual container.
4. Primary body text should be #E4EEFF (cool blue-white). Currently rendering as muted gray. Check that text-primary token is applied consistently.
5. Hero section: 'Before you register.' subtitle is too visually quiet — same weight as secondary text. Both headline lines should feel bold and confident. Consider making the subtitle slightly smaller but full-opacity, not muted.
6. Input bar at the bottom of /chat: wrap in a surface container (#0C1220) with a top border (#172035) to separate it from the page. Currently it floats without grounding.

description:
Deck list generation from the chat feature always produces empty sideboards. Root cause is likely upstream: MTGO's decklists page does not publish sideboard data, so deck_cards has 0 sideboard entries in the DB (confirmed — this has been a known limitation since Phase 1).

Investigation required:
1. Confirm deck_cards sideboard count is actually 0 in prod: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM deck_cards WHERE is_sideboard = true (or equivalent)
2. Check whether MTGGoldfish scraper (mindsculptor-aws) or MTGTop8 scraper (mindsculptor-397) ingest sideboard data — these may be viable sources
3. If sideboard data exists from non-MTGO sources, verify the retrieval layer (src/query/retrieval.ts) is not filtering it out
4. If no sideboard data exists in DB at all, the fix is in the ingestion pipeline: identify which scrape source has sideboards and parse them into deck_cards with the sideboard flag set

Acceptance: generated deck lists include a valid 15-card sideboard drawn from real tournament data.
title: Bug: deck list exports have empty sideboards

description:
## Overview

Add TCGPlayer (including ChannelFireball) as a second article source alongside MTGGoldfish. TCGPlayer hosts high-quality competitive strategy content from pro players (Arne Huschenbeth, Frank Karsten, Reid Duke, etc.) — exactly the expert analysis our oracle needs.

## API Discovery

TCGPlayer's content platform exposes an undocumented JSON API at `infinite-api.tcgplayer.com`:

### Listing endpoint
`GET https://infinite-api.tcgplayer.com/c/articles?vertical=magic&rows=N`

Returns paginated article metadata:
- `title`, `authorName`, `dateTime`, `uuid`
- `format` (e.g. `"Standard"`) — structured, no regex guessing needed
- `tags` (e.g. `["competitive"]`), `internalTags` (e.g. `["channelfireball"]`)
- `restrictions` — `"none"` for free articles (filter out paywalled content)
- `canonicalURL` for dedup
- `vertical` filter keeps results to Magic only
- Total corpus: ~20k articles

### Single article endpoint
`GET https://infinite-api.tcgplayer.com/c/article/{uuid}`

Returns full article with HTML body:
- `body` field contains complete article HTML
- Card names are in structured `<card-hover-link card-name="..." />` elements — trivially parseable, no brute-force Scryfall matching needed
- Deck embeds via `<deck-embed deck-id="..." />` tags
- `restrictions` field to confirm article is free
- Author bio, read time, updated timestamp

## Requirements

### 1. Scraper: `src/scrapers/tcgplayer-articles.ts`

- Fetch article listings from `/c/articles?vertical=magic&rows=50` with pagination
- Filter to `restrictions: "none"` (free articles only)
- Filter to relevant tags: `competitive`, `strategy`, `deck guide`, etc. — skip finance, unboxing, product news
- Deduplicate against `articles` table by URL (use `canonicalURL` to build full URL)
- For each new article, fetch full body from `/c/article/{uuid}`
- Rate limit: 2s between requests (same as MTGGoldfish)
- Insert into `articles` table with `source: 'tcgplayer'`
- Format field comes directly from API response (lowercase it to match our convention)
- Extract author from API response (no HTML parsing needed)

### 2. Parser: `src/parsers/tcgplayer-articles.ts`

- Extract card names from `<card-hover-link card-name="...">` and `<card-showcase ids="...">` elements — these are structured and reliable, use them as the primary card extraction method
- Fall back to brute-force Scryfall name matching for any unlinked mentions (reuse existing `extractCardNames` from mtggoldfish parser, or extract shared utility)
- Chunk article body using existing `chunkArticle()` from mtggoldfish parser (reuse, don't duplicate)
- Tag archetypes using existing `extractArchetypes()` (reuse)
- Embed chunks via Voyage AI (reuse existing embed pipeline)
- Store in `article_chunks` with same schema

### 3. Worker: `src/workers/sync-tcgplayer-articles.ts`

- Simple cron entrypoint mirroring `sync-articles.ts`
- `npm run sync:tcgplayer-articles` script in package.json

### 4. Cron in `render.yaml`

- `sync-tcgplayer-articles` cron job
- Schedule: weekly, offset from MTGGoldfish sync (e.g. `0 5 * * 1` — Monday 5am UTC)
- Needs `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`

### 5. Structured error logging / API health monitoring

The TCGPlayer API is undocumented and could change without notice. Add structured error logging that will alert us if the scraper breaks:

- **Response shape validation**: after fetching from `/c/articles` and `/c/article/{uuid}`, validate that the response contains expected fields (`result`, `body`, `title`, `dateTime`, etc.). If the shape changes, log a structured error like:
  ```
  [tcgplayer-articles] API_SCHEMA_CHANGE: /c/articles response missing field "result" — expected array, got undefined
  ```
- **HTTP status monitoring**: log non-200 responses with the endpoint and status code:
  ```
  [tcgplayer-articles] API_ERROR: GET /c/articles returned HTTP 403
  ```
- **Empty result detection**: if `/c/articles` returns 0 results when we expect articles, log:
  ```
  [tcgplayer-articles] API_EMPTY: /c/articles returned 0 articles — API may have changed or be rate-limiting
  ```
- **Success metrics**: at the end of each run, log a structured summary:
  ```
  [tcgplayer-articles] SYNC_COMPLETE: new=12, skipped=38, errors=0, api_failures=0
  ```
- Apply the same structured logging to the existing MTGGoldfish scraper for consistency

### 6. Shared utilities

Factor out shared code between MTGGoldfish and TCGPlayer parsers:
- `chunkArticle()` — already generic, just re-export or move to a shared module
- `extractArchetypes()` — same
- Card name cache loading — same
- Voyage embedding — same

Keep it minimal. If it's just re-exporting from the MTGGoldfish parser, that's fine. Don't create a premature abstraction layer.

### 7. Tests: `src/parsers/__tests__/tcgplayer-articles.test.ts`

- Card name extraction from `<card-hover-link>` elements
- Card name extraction from `<card-showcase ids="...">` elements
- Handles articles with no card links gracefully
- Format normalization (API returns "Standard", we store "standard")
- Free article filtering (respects `restrictions` field)

### 8. Backfill

- After the scraper is working, run an initial backfill to pull recent competitive Magic articles (last 2-3 months)
- Verify chunks look correct, embeddings are populated, card names are extracted

## NOT doing

- Ingesting paywalled/premium articles
- Deck embed resolution (the `<deck-embed>` tags reference TCGPlayer deck IDs — we don't need to resolve these, the surrounding article text has enough context)
- Ingesting non-Magic content (Yu-Gi-Oh, Flesh and Blood, etc.)
- Building a shared "article scraper framework" — keep MTGGoldfish and TCGPlayer as separate, simple scrapers that share utility functions

## Verification

1. `npx tsc --noEmit`
2. `npm test`
3. Run scraper locally, verify articles + chunks + embeddings in DB
4. Test oracle query that should surface TCGPlayer content (e.g. "What are pros saying about Standard Izzet Blink?")
5. Self-audit all changed files
title: TCGPlayer/ChannelFireball article ingestion

description: attachDeckCosts computes minCoverage = totalQty * 0.75. A deck with 24 basics (no price) + 36 spells (priced) hits 60% coverage and returns null cost correctly. But if 40 cheap cantrips (priced) + 20 expensive lands (not priced) → 67% coverage, null returned correctly. The problem is inverse: 40 priced spells + 4 fetch lands (unpriced, high cost) = 91% coverage threshold passed but cost is missing 60-80% of actual value. Fix: null out cost when any card slot with estimated value >  is unpriced, or more practically, check coverage against the number of *unique card names* not raw quantity.
title: retrieval: deck cost coverage check is quantity-based not value-based — 75% of card copies having prices can still miss most of the cost

description: mtgtop8.ts line 83 and mtggoldfish equivalent: if no date found in HTML, defaults to new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]. This silently attributes an old tournament to today, inflating recency scores in snapshots and retrieval. Should skip the tournament or set date to null (if column allows) rather than today.
title: parsers: date fallback should be null not today — undated tournaments silently land in today's recency window

description:
## Root Cause

In `src/app/chat/page.tsx`:

```ts
const [remaining, setRemaining] = useState<number | null>(null)
...
const used = isAnon ? anonCount : (remaining != null ? limit - remaining : 0)
const displayRemaining = limit - used
```

`remaining` initializes to `null`. Before any query is made, `used` falls through to `0`, so `displayRemaining = USER_LIMIT - 0 = 10`. The true count is only fetched as a side-effect of making a query (via the `rate_limit` field in the SSE `meta` event). Hard refresh drops that state.

## Fix

Add a `useEffect` that fetches the user's current daily usage from `/api/query-status` (or inline via Supabase browser client) on mount, conditional on `user` being non-null.

### Option A — dedicated API route (preferred)

New route: `GET /api/query-status`
- Reads auth from cookies (same as the existing query route)
- Queries `oracle_queries` table for today's count for this user
- Returns `{ remaining: number, resets_at: string }`

In `ChatPageInner`, after auth state resolves:
```ts
useEffect(() => {
  if (!user) return
  fetch('/api/query-status')
    .then(r => r.json())
    .then(d => {
      setRemaining(d.remaining)
      setResetsAt(d.resets_at)
    })
    .catch(() => {}) // best effort, don't block UI
}, [user])
```

### Option B — Supabase browser client query (simpler, no new route)

Query `oracle_queries` directly from the browser client on mount. Avoids the extra route but couples the client to the DB schema.

Option A is preferred — keeps DB logic server-side, consistent with the rest of the query pipeline.

## Notes

- The fetch should only run when `user` is set. Anon counter already uses localStorage (no DB), so this only affects logged-in users.
- Show a loading skeleton or leave the counter blank (`—`) until the fetch resolves, rather than showing `10` as a false default. This avoids the jarring flash.
- If the fetch fails, default to `USER_LIMIT` (optimistic) and don't block the UI.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Hard refresh while logged in shows accurate remaining count within ~500ms, not `10`
- [ ] Counter blank or skeleton while fetch is in-flight (no false `10`)
- [ ] Anon behavior unchanged
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
title: Hard refresh shows stale '10 queries left' instead of real count

description:
## Reported Bug

User flow: sign in → exhaust 10 logged-in queries → sign out → receive 5 fresh anonymous queries. Effective total: 15 queries with zero friction. The anon and auth rate limit systems are completely independent and share no state.

## Root Cause Summary

There are two parallel rate limit systems with no connection:
- **Anon**: `localStorage` key `fm_anon_queries` → `{ count, date }`. Purely client-side. No server enforcement.
- **Auth**: `oracle_queries` DB table, enforced server-side. Count returned via SSE `meta` event.

Signing out clears auth session state but leaves localStorage untouched (or resets `user` to null in React state, making `isAnon = true`). The anon bucket is then fresh (or refills at midnight independent of auth bucket). There is no mechanism that says "this identity has already used X queries today."

---

## Full State Audit

### Legitimate states the system must handle correctly

| # | Flow | Expected | Actual |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Anon → exhaust 5 → sign in | Carry over used count — auth limit should be 10 − (proportion or just 10 fresh) | Gets full 10 fresh auth queries |
| 2 | Auth → exhaust 10 → sign out | Anon bucket should be 0 (or locked) for remainder of day | Gets 5 fresh anon queries ← **reported bug** |
| 3 | Auth → exhaust 10 → sign out → sign back in (same user) | Still exhausted | Client `remaining` resets to `null` → shows 10 until next query (agd bug), server correctly returns 429 |
| 4 | Auth → exhaust 10 → sign out → sign in as different Google account | Gets another 10 | This is acceptable; different identity |
| 5 | Anon → exhaust 5 → open incognito tab | Gets 5 more | localStorage is per-tab-context; this is expected and acceptable |
| 6 | Anon → 3 queries → sign in (OAuth popup) | Anon count preserved in localStorage; user now on auth limit | Auth limit starts fresh with no credit for 3 anon queries used |
| 7 | Anon → 3 queries → sign in (OAuth redirect fallback) | Same as above | Redirect clears page state; localStorage persists; anon count still 3 but irrelevant post-auth |
| 8 | Auth → in-flight query → sign out | Query completes; response renders on now-anon session | `user` becomes null mid-stream; `isAnon` flips; meta footer may show wrong remaining count |
| 9 | Tab crash after response but before localStorage write | Anon count not incremented; user gets extra query on reload | Client-side increment is best-effort; acceptable |
| 10 | Direct API call (bypass frontend) | Anon: no server enforcement — unlimited. Auth: server-enforced via DB | Anon has zero server-side protection |
| 11 | Multiple browser tabs, same anon session | Each tab reads/writes localStorage; race condition on concurrent submits | Two tabs can each submit a 5th query simultaneously before either increments count |
| 12 | Midnight UTC rollover mid-session | Counters reset; user who hit limit can now query again | Correct by design; both systems use UTC date key |

---

## Edge Cases to Resolve

### P0 within this audit: Sign-out bypass (reported bug)
When a logged-in user signs out, the anon bucket must not grant fresh queries for the remainder of the day. Options:
- **Option A**: On sign-out, set anon localStorage count to `ANON_LIMIT` (5) for today — effectively locks anon. Simple. No server call needed.
- **Option B**: Write a `used_today` flag to localStorage on sign-out, checked by anon pre-check. More explicit than manipulating the count.
- **Option C**: Server-side anon tracking via IP or a device fingerprint. Heavier, privacy implications, overkill for current scale.

**Recommended: Option A.** On `signOut()` in Nav.tsx, after `supabase.auth.signOut()`, write `{ count: ANON_LIMIT, date: getTodayUTC() }` to `localStorage[ANON_STORAGE_KEY]`. This collapses the anon bucket for the day. Requires sharing the storage key constant between Nav.tsx and chat/page.tsx — extract to a shared constants file.

### Anon → sign-in: should used anon queries count against auth limit?
Currently: anon and auth are siloed — signing in resets to 10 regardless of prior anon usage. Arguable either way. **Recommendation: leave as-is for now.** Carrying over anon count is complex (requires passing localStorage state through the OAuth flow) and the conversion incentive of a "fresh" higher limit is useful. Document as a known intentional behavior.

### Server-side anon enforcement gap
Anon rate limiting is purely client-side. Any user who knows the `/api/query` endpoint can bypass it entirely. **This is out of scope for the current V0 rate limiting** (bead mindsculptor-toy.15 was explicitly scoped to client-side anon enforcement). Flag for toy.7 (server-side enforcement) which already exists in the backlog. Add a note there.

### Tab race condition (concurrent anon submits)
Two tabs can each pass the `current < ANON_LIMIT` check before either increments. Low probability in practice (user would need two tabs both at the limit simultaneously). Accept as a known limitation for V0.

### In-flight query on sign-out
When user signs out mid-stream, `user` becomes null, `isAnon` flips to true. The SSE stream is still reading. The meta footer will show anon remaining count instead of auth. Minor cosmetic issue; the response still renders. Accept for now, low priority.

---

## Required Changes

1. **Extract rate limit constants to shared file** (`src/lib/rate-limit-constants.ts` or similar): `ANON_LIMIT`, `USER_LIMIT`, `ANON_STORAGE_KEY`, `CHAT_STORAGE_KEY`, `getTodayUTC()`. Currently duplicated across chat/page.tsx and potentially other files.

2. **Sign-out: lock anon bucket** — in `Nav.tsx` `signOut()`, after auth sign-out completes, write exhausted anon count to localStorage for today.

3. **Audit all sign-out/sign-in state transitions in chat/page.tsx** — verify `remaining`, `anonCount`, `showAuthCard`, `resetsAt` all update correctly when auth state changes mid-session (the `onAuthStateChange` listener exists but only sets `user`; derived state doesn't recompute).

4. **Document the known acceptable gaps**: anon multi-tab, anon→auth carry-over, API bypass — as intentional V0 limitations with beads/notes for future enforcement phases.

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sign in → exhaust queries → sign out → anon bucket is locked for the day (0 remaining)
- [ ] Sign out mid-session → anon counter shows 0, input disabled
- [ ] Sign in mid-anon-session → remaining shows auth limit (10), not anon limit
- [ ] Rate limit constants are in one shared location, not duplicated
- [ ] All 12 flows in the state audit table manually tested and documented
- [ ] Known acceptable gaps documented in code comments
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
title: Rate limiting state audit — sign-out bypass and cross-identity edge cases
Offensive security test suite + defenses — rate limit attack vectors

description:
## Context

Mobile is a first-class experience. This bead is a comprehensive audit and fix pass — not a quick tweak. Every page and component must be reviewed at 375px (iPhone SE) and 390px (iPhone 14). The outcome must be a UI that feels intentional at all viewport sizes, not just "doesn't break."

---

## Known Issues (spotted in code review)

### 1. Nav — critical overflow
`Nav.tsx`: a single `flex` row with logo + two NavLinks + icon buttons + "Go Spike ↑" button in `px-6 h-12`. At 375px this overflows immediately. "Metagame Data" alone is ~110px, the full row is 450-500px in a 375px container.

**Fix: mobile nav collapse**
- Breakpoint: `md:` (768px) shows the current desktop layout
- Below `md`: show logo + hamburger icon on the right; hide NavLinks, icon buttons, Go Spike
- Hamburger opens a full-width slide-down or overlay menu with: Chat, Metagame Data, separator, Sign in / account, Go Spike
- Keep the sticky bar + backdrop blur — just collapse the items

### 2. MetaShareBar recharts — broken on mobile
`MetaShareBar.tsx`: `margin={{ left: 160, right: 20 }}` is fixed. At 375px − 48px padding = 327px content → 327 − 160 − 20 = **147px for bars**. That's survivable but the `CustomYTick` `foreignObject` is `width={200} x={-200}`, meaning the archetype name labels render outside the SVG bounds and clip.

**Fix: responsive left margin**
- Use a `useWindowWidth` or measure container width with `useRef` + `ResizeObserver`
- On `< 640px`: collapse to showing only top 8 archetypes (not 15), reduce left margin to 110px, shorten archetype names to first 14 chars + ellipsis
- Alternative: swap the chart to a vertical list layout (plain div rows with a CSS bar) below `sm:` — avoids recharts SVG complexity entirely on mobile and is more touch-friendly

The vertical list approach is preferred: renders identical data as CSS `<div style={{ width: `${pct}%` }}>` bars, links intact, no foreignObject clipping. Recharts chart remains for `md:` and up.

### 3. RecentResults table — horizontal scroll is poor UX
6-column table with `overflow-x-auto`. On mobile users don't discover horizontal scroll naturally, and the table is too dense.

**Fix: responsive table / card layout**
- Below `sm:`: render results as stacked cards instead of table rows
- Each card: placement (large, colored) + event name (linked) + date + tier badge + pilot + record in a compact 2-column grid
- `sm:` and up: current table layout

### 4. Chat page — suggested prompts grid
`grid grid-cols-2 gap-3` is always 2 columns. At 375px each button is ~150px wide. Prompts like "What should I play at my RCQ this weekend?" will truncate.

**Fix:** `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 gap-3` — stack on mobile, 2-col from sm up.

### 5. Chat input bar — padding and button size
`px-6 py-4` with `flex gap-3` Input + Button. At 375px: 375 − 48px padding = 327px. Input + "Ask" button in 327px is manageable but tight. Button should be `shrink-0` (already is in OracleInput but check chat page). The `py-4` padding on the sticky input bar consumes significant vertical space on mobile — consider `py-3` on mobile.

### 6. Landing page CTAs
`flex items-center gap-4` with two buttons side by side. At 375px: "Ask the Firemind — it's free" button + "See metagame charts →" will either overflow or wrap awkwardly.

**Fix:** `flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center gap-3`

### 7. Archetype detail — header stat row
`flex items-baseline gap-4 flex-wrap` — wraps fine. But `px-6 py-12` outer padding: `py-12` is 48px top/bottom. On mobile that's a lot. Consider `py-8 md:py-12`.

### 8. Format overview — range selector
Range buttons (`30d / 60d / 90d`) are rendered in a `flex gap-2`. If the format page header stats row is also in a flex row, it may overflow. Check at 375px.

### 9. TrendLines chart (recharts LineChart)
Has `margin={{ top: 5, right: 10, left: -20, bottom: 5 }}`. The negative left margin and small right margin may cause axis labels to clip on narrow screens. Review at 375px.

### 10. Touch targets
Audit every button/link for 44×44px minimum touch target (WCAG 2.5.5). Known small targets:
- NavLink pills: `text-sm px-3 py-1.5` → ~36px tall
- Range selector buttons: same
- "Go Spike ↑" button: `px-3 py-1.5` → ~36px tall
- MetaShareBar archetype name links inside SVG foreignObject — likely untappable

---

## Viewport Test Matrix

Every fix must be verified at:
| Width | Device |
|---|---|
| 375px | iPhone SE / older iPhones |
| 390px | iPhone 14 Pro |
| 414px | iPhone 14 Plus |
| 768px | iPad mini portrait |
| 1024px | iPad landscape / small laptop |
| 1280px+ | Desktop (must not regress) |

---

## Pages to Audit

1. `/` — Landing page
2. `/chat` — Chat (empty state + active state with messages)
3. `/data/modern` — Format overview (MetaShareBar, TopMovers, TrendLines)
4. `/data/modern/[archetype]` — Archetype detail (header stats, RecentResults table, ShareOverTime, Oracle CTAs)
5. Nav (all states: logged out, logged in, active path)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Nav does not overflow at 375px; hamburger menu opens/closes cleanly
- [ ] MetaShareBar is readable and usable at 375px (either responsive margins or list layout swap)
- [ ] RecentResults table has card layout below `sm:`
- [ ] Chat suggested prompts stack to 1 column below `sm:`
- [ ] Landing CTAs stack vertically on mobile
- [ ] All interactive elements meet 44px minimum touch target height
- [ ] No horizontal page overflow at 375px on any page (test with `overflow-x: hidden` on body, nothing clips weirdly)
- [ ] Charts (ShareOverTime, TrendLines) are legible at 375px — axis labels not clipping, bars not invisible
- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes
- [ ] Visually tested at all widths in the test matrix above
title: Mobile responsiveness audit and fix — comprehensive pass

description:
## Goal

Ingest strategy articles from MTGGoldfish into chunked, embedded form so the oracle query pipeline can retrieve expert analysis alongside tournament data. This is Phase A — data pipeline only, no retrieval changes yet.

## Sources

MTGGoldfish articles only (deck techs, sideboard guides, metagame breakdowns). They use real experts and cover both Modern and Standard consistently.

## Schema

### `articles` table
- `id` uuid PK
- `source` text NOT NULL (e.g. 'mtggoldfish')
- `url` text UNIQUE NOT NULL
- `title` text NOT NULL
- `author` text
- `published_at` timestamptz NOT NULL
- `format` text (nullable — 'modern', 'standard', or null if cross-format)
- `scraped_at` timestamptz DEFAULT now()

### `article_chunks` table
- `id` uuid PK
- `article_id` uuid FK → articles(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
- `chunk_index` int NOT NULL
- `content` text NOT NULL
- `embedding` vector(1024) — Voyage-3
- `archetypes` text[] — extracted archetype mentions for filtered retrieval
- `cards_mentioned` text[] — extracted card names for glossary join-back
- UNIQUE (article_id, chunk_index)
- IVFFlat index on embedding

## Scraper

- Scrape MTGGoldfish article index pages to discover new articles
- For each new article (URL not in `articles` table): fetch, extract title/author/date/format/body
- Store chunked excerpts only — NOT full article text (copyright risk mitigation)
- Chunking: split on section headers (most deck techs have clear H2/H3 structure), fall back to ~500 token sliding window with ~50 token overlap
- Per chunk: extract card names (regex against Scryfall `cards` table names) and archetype mentions
- Embed each chunk via Voyage-3

## Cadence

Weekly Render cron (same pattern as Scryfall sync). Articles don't change post-publication.

## Deliverables

1. Supabase migration for `articles` + `article_chunks` tables
2. `src/scrapers/mtggoldfish-articles.ts` — article discovery + fetch
3. `src/parsers/mtggoldfish-articles.ts` — chunking, card/archetype extraction, embedding
4. `src/workers/sync-articles.ts` — cron entrypoint
5. render.yaml entry for weekly cron
6. Tests for chunking logic and card name extraction

## NOT in scope
- Retrieval changes (Phase B)
- Assembly/prompt changes (Phase B)
- Sources beyond MTGGoldfish
- Full article text storage
title: Article ingestion pipeline — MTGGoldfish scraper, chunking, and embeddings

description:
## Problem

The chat UI has no conversation continuity. Each message is an isolated single-turn LLM call — no history is sent anywhere in the stack.

Concrete failure: user asked a question, Firemind responded with some incorrect card details, user corrected those details in a follow-up message. Firemind agreed but said 'whoever claimed those things was wrong' — because it had zero memory of being the one who said them two messages ago.

## Root cause

The entire pipeline is stateless:
- Chat UI sends `{ query: string }` only — no history
- API route: `handleQuery(body.query)` — single string, no history
- `handleQuery` calls `llm.complete(system, user)` — one-shot completion
- `llm.complete` signature is `(system, user, opts)` — no messages array

## What to build

Thread the last 6 messages (3 user + 3 oracle exchanges) as conversation history through the full stack. Last 6 is chosen to cover immediate correction loops without unbounded token growth.

**Intent extraction stays single-turn** — it runs on only the current query string, not history. Full history in the intent prompt would be noisy and expensive.

### Changes required

1. `src/lib/llm.ts` — add `completeWithHistory(system, history, opts)` where history is `{ role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string }[]`. Keep existing `complete()` for single-turn uses (intent extraction, etc).

2. `src/query/index.ts` — `handleQuery(query, history?)` accepts optional prior messages. Passes history to the LLM call via `completeWithHistory`. History should NOT be passed to `extractIntent` — intent extraction stays single-turn on the current query.

3. `src/app/api/query/route.ts` — accept optional `messages` array in the request body alongside `query`. Validate shape, pass to `handleQuery`. Truncate to last 6 messages server-side as a safety bound.

4. `src/app/chat/page.tsx` — on each submit, send the current `messages` state (last 6, excluding the message being submitted) as `messages` in the POST body. Map oracle role to 'assistant' for the API.

### History format sent to API
```ts
{
  query: string,
  messages?: { role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string }[]
}
```

### LLM call shape
The Claude API messages array should be:
  [...history, { role: 'user', content: currentUserMessage }]
where currentUserMessage is the assembled context + query (same as current single-turn user message).

## Acceptance criteria
- Follow-up corrections work: model knows it was the one who made prior claims
- Follow-up questions work: 'which of those is cheapest?' after a deck list response works correctly
- No history → behavior identical to today (backward compatible)
- Intent extraction is NOT affected (still single-turn)
- History capped at 6 messages server-side regardless of what client sends
- Existing tests pass
title: Thread conversation history through the query pipeline
snapshot fetchMetaShares should filter by method='jaccard' to avoid cross-method double counting

description:
## Goal

Retrieve relevant article chunks during oracle queries and include them in the LLM context as an "Expert Analysis" section. This gives the LLM expert reasoning (sideboard rationale, matchup theory, card evaluations) instead of guessing from decklists alone.

## Depends on

mindsculptor-hpa (article ingestion pipeline) must be complete — articles and chunks must exist in DB with embeddings.

## Retrieval changes (`src/query/retrieval.ts`)

### New function: `fetchRelevantArticles(intent: Intent)`

1. Embed the user query via Voyage (already have the integration)
2. Vector search `article_chunks` filtered by:
   - `format` matching `intent.format` (or null for cross-format articles)
   - Recency: weight by `articles.published_at` — prefer articles within `intent.timeframe_days`, but allow slightly older if few recent results. Recency weighting is critical — a 2-month-old sideboard guide may be stale after a meta shift.
   - Optionally filter by `archetypes` array overlap if `intent.archetype` or `intent.opponent_archetype` is set
3. Return top 5-8 chunks, deduplicated by article (max 2-3 chunks per article to avoid one article dominating)
4. Include article metadata (title, author, date, source) alongside chunk content

### Wire into `retrieveContext`

Add `fetchRelevantArticles` as a parallel call alongside `fetchTopDecks`, `fetchCardInfo`, `fetchCardGlossary`. Add result to `RetrievedData` as `article_chunks: ArticleChunk[]`.

### Card glossary enrichment

Extract `cards_mentioned` from retrieved article chunks and merge into the `additionalNames` passed to `fetchCardGlossary` — so any card an expert article discusses also gets Scryfall oracle data in the glossary.

## Assembly changes (`src/query/assemble.ts`)

### New context section

```
=== Expert Analysis ===
[MTGGoldfish - "Burn Sideboard Guide for Modern" by Frank Karsten, 2026-02-15]
Against Izzet Control, the key cards to bring in are...

[MTGGoldfish - "Modern Metagame Breakdown" by SaffronOlive, 2026-02-20]
Monument to Endurance has been overperforming...
```

Include author + date + source for attribution and so the LLM can weight recency.

### System prompt update

Add guideline to `RESPONSE_SYSTEM_BASE`:
```
- Expert Analysis: snippets from published strategy articles are provided when available. Cite the author and article title when referencing expert opinions. Prefer recent articles over older ones when advice conflicts. Tournament results (actual placements) take precedence over article opinions when they disagree.
```

## Context window budget

Include full article chunks for now — do not trim tournament data to make room. Monitor token usage and narrow down if costs get too high. If trimming is needed later, reduce article chunk count first (they're supplementary to the core tournament data).

## Deliverables

1. `RetrievedData` type updated with `article_chunks` field
2. `fetchRelevantArticles()` in retrieval.ts
3. `assembleContext` updated with Expert Analysis section
4. System prompt hardened with article citation guidelines
5. Tests for article retrieval and assembly formatting
6. Self-audit of context window size with real queries

## NOT in scope
- Additional article sources (Reddit, CFB, SCG)
- Article quality/author tier weighting (future enhancement)
- Context window trimming logic (only if empirically needed)
